title: Formidable Red Sox extend win streak to 11 games with doubleheader sweep of Rays
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox extended their winning streak to 11 games after sweeping a doubleheader against the Tampa Bay Rays.
In Game 1, the Red Sox dominated 10-0 with strong pitching from Jake Bennett and multiple RBI hits.
Game 2 saw Boston overcome a 2-0 deficit, winning 5-3 with home runs from Wilyer Abreu and Willson Contreras.

title: Chad Tracy offers updates on several injured Red Sox pitchers
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Ranger Suarez, on the 15-day IL, might start on July 21 after recovering from a left groin strain.
Connelly Early is progressing well with his left elbow inflammation and resumed throwing, though no return date is set.
Tanner Houck is throwing twice a week from the mound as he recovers from Tommy John surgery, aiming for 2026 return.

title: 4 Red Sox who rose their stock following Friday’s doubleheader sweep vs. Rays
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Masataka Yoshida hit a home run and had three hits, boosting the Red Sox in Game 1 vs. Rays.
Caleb Durbin went 5 for 7 at the plate with an RBI and two runs, pressuring Tampa Bay's pitchers.
Jake Bennett pitched 6 scoreless innings, allowing one hit and one walk, aiding Boston's doubleheader sweep.

title: Robert Kraft backs Mike Vrabel after controversial offseason: ‘No one is infallible’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Robert Kraft supports Mike Vrabel as Patriots head coach despite controversial offseason involving Dianna Russini.
Vrabel denied an affair and missed Day 3 of the NFL draft; Russini resigned from The Athletic.
Patriots training camp begins next Saturday at Gillette Stadium, with Vrabel remaining as head coach.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Anya Taylor-Joy stars in 'Lucky,' a new crime series now streaming on Apple TV.
'Obsession' is a surprise horror hit streaming on Peacock, grossing over $400 million.
'The Hunt for the Wilderpeople,' featuring the late Sam Neill, is available on Prime Video and Plex.

title: NH judge: Concord man violated Civil Rights Act in assault on transgender woman
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Travis Lufkin was found guilty of violating the NH Civil Rights Act by assaulting a transgender woman in Concord.
The court ordered Lufkin to avoid the victim and imposed a $5,000 fine, with $4,000 suspended.
Lufkin pleaded guilty to assault charges, receiving a 12-month sentence with part suspension contingent on compliance.

title: Red Sox’ trade deadline plans ‘fluid,’ ESPN’s Jeff Passan says
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox have won 10 consecutive games and aim to reach .500 against the Tampa Bay Rays.
Red Sox' trade deadline plans remain unclear, with potential moves being considered by chief baseball officer Craig Breslow.
Despite rumors, trading closer Aroldis Chapman is increasingly unlikely as playoff odds improve to 44.3%, says Jon Morosi.

title: Popular Nantucket pizzeria Pi is for sale for $12.5 million
author: Madison Lucchesi
bullets: Nantucket's Pi Pizzeria, opened by Evan and Maria Marley in 2001, is for sale for $12.5 million.
The sale includes the restaurant, a wood fire pizza oven, a 46-seat dining room, and staff housing.
Evan Marley plans to assist in the transition to ensure continuity for the established business.

title: France’s World Cup squad bids adieu to Boston
author: John Waller
bullets: France's national soccer team left Boston, ending their stay at the Four Seasons Hotel since early June.
The team heads to Miami for World Cup third place match against England after losing 2-0 to Spain.
While in Boston, the France team practiced at Bentley University in Waltham.

title: Matt Damon says he’s working on a Karen Read movie
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Matt Damon announced that his production company is developing a movie about Karen Read's murder case.
Karen Read was acquitted of charges related to the death of Boston Police Officer John O'Keefe in 2022.
Multiple projects, including films and series, are planned around Read's controversial case across various platforms.
